subject: use docker-compose healtcheck for DB
commit_message:
use docker-compose healtcheck for DB

and use better condition for starting the other
dockers
taking into account Chris Lott comments

Change-Id: Ibfdbd26742e514c107532ae99b5c4fd2d05c1e2c
Issue-ID: PORTAL-95
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

I tested it manually just to see what happens and it gives an error:

    # mysql ping -h localhost
    ERROR 1045 (28000): Access denied for user 'root'@'localhost' (using password: NO)

Is this expected?

Patch Set 1:

> I tested it manually just to see what happens and mysqladmin gives an
 > error:
 > 
 > # mysqladmin ping -h localhost
 > ERROR 1045 (28000): Access denied for user 'root'@'localhost'
 > (using password: NO)
 > 
 > Does this result still mean the healthcheck was successful?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I'm a little concerned that you have used a feature of version 2 that will not be supported in the future, according to the docker-compose doc:

    Version 3 no longer supports the condition form of depends_on.

Please advise if there's a way to do this that will gracefully continue working if & when we migrate to docker-compose version 3.

Patch Set 1:

Hey Chris, sorry for late answer.

On the manual test, my bad, I just checked it was "helthy" on docker ps and not that the command was actually returning something. The important stuff in an healthcheck test is the return code and the return code is 0 even with the output seen, meaning that it's working as expected.

On the long term existence of v2 of docker-compose, v3 is a transtion path to docker swarm. That means that it's interesting only if we want to use swarm later.
AFAIK, there's a kubernetes project in parallel of 'heat' project. So either we'll continue on heat (and on v2.x docker compose file reference), either we'll move to k8s (and then we don't really care...)

Hopes that clarifies!

Patch Set 1:

Hi Sylvain Desbureaux, Thanks for the change. Our team had analyzed the change and we agree that we should have this enhancement. However, we identified it as low priority and planning to apply this in Beijing release. Please see, if you can enter a new Jira item for this change on Portal, so that it can be tracked.

Patch Set 1:

Sylvain, I have tested this successfully! My change also removes the wait-for shell script which is no longer required.  I can try to add my change to this revision request - is that possible?

Patch Set 1:

Chris, you can do it if you want no problem!
